title: no forcing function for warranted non-invariant closure-table edges: edge presence is enforced only for requires-edge-backed relation_types, so warranted task_governed_by_decision / task_addresses_gap / research_informs_item edges are omitted with nothing to flag the absence
canonical_vocabulary: forcing-function coverage for warranted non-invariant closure-table edges
description: Closure-table edge presence is enforced only for relation_types backed by a requires-edge invariant: completed-task-has-verification, decision-cites-forcing-artifact, the three *-articulates-convention invariants, and decision-shows-derivation. The vocabulary-free requires-edge loop (the mechanism DEC-0016 establishes, context-sdk.ts:2055-2078) flags at context-validate any item in the invariant's block missing an edge of the named relation_types. Every other warranted relation_type — task_governed_by_decision, task_addresses_gap, task_addresses_feature, research_informs_item, the data_flow links — has no requires-edge invariant and is filed only by a manual append-relation step that runs after the item write; no surface flags a warranted-but-absent non-invariant edge. The forcing-function absence is both-surface: write-time has no completeness check tying an item's filed body to the edges its text entails, and context-validate has no rule flagging a missing non-invariant edge. Warranted means entailed by an item's filed text or by the relation_type's intended population — not universal (not every task addresses a gap), so a blanket requires-edge invariant is not the answer for every type. Edges an item's own text entails are written inconsistently or omitted, concentrated in pre-discipline history. General class: the five missing TASK->decision/gap edges (TASK-010->DEC-0008 and ->FGAP-012, TASK-012->DEC-0009 and ->FGAP-015, TASK-014->FGAP-014) are the triggering instance; FGAP-071 (gap->arc binding) is a contained sibling instance already filed narrowly.

evidence.reference: read-config --registry invariants: six requires-edge invariants (completed-task-has-verification, decision-cites-forcing-artifact, decision/feature/task-articulates-convention, decision-shows-derivation); the status-consistency invariants compare buckets on an already-existing edge and never require edge presence
read-config --registry relation_types: 38 relation_types; only the six requires-edge-named ones are forced; task_governed_by_decision / task_addresses_gap / research_informs_item carry no invariant
context-validate: 17 decision-shows-derivation warnings (an invariant-backed absence IS flagged) and zero warnings for any absent task_governed_by_decision / task_addresses_gap edge — the asymmetry
find-references TASK-010: edges VER-009 + two conventions; no edge to DEC-0008 or FGAP-012 though TASK-010 description names 'Per DEC-0008 / FGAP-012'
find-references TASK-012: edges VER-007 + two conventions; no edge to DEC-0009 or FGAP-015 though TASK-012 is FGAP-013's fix and DEC-0009's enacting task
find-references TASK-014: edges VER-008 + one convention; no edge to FGAP-014 though TASK-014 description names 'Closes FGAP-014'

the grounded investigation; page-through of all 90 framework-gaps found no item titling the class; TASK-041 + FGAP-071 are per-type instances, DEC-0016 the mechanism, FGAP-090 / FGAP-007 adjacent edge-write axes

impact: The closure-table graph is systematically incomplete for non-invariant relations: task_governed_by_decision, task_addresses_gap, research_informs_item are inconsistently populated. Any consumer reading the graph for provenance or derivation (gate-aware derivation FGAP-061, lineage walks, 'what addressed this gap') gets a partial answer, and the absences are invisible — no flag — so they accumulate silently as item counts grow; the flat-list-drift pattern FGAP-071 names for gaps, generalized to all warranted edges.
proposed_resolution: To be determined exactly from live codebase.
